frameworks.name: retinotopic organization
frameworks.one_line: low-level spatial mapping that dominates early visual cortex

frameworks.name: shape-curvature representation
frameworks.one_line: abstract shape coding based on curvature features in lateral occipital cortex

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: multivariate fMRI patterns compared against stimulus similarity models

core_question: Where along the lateral occipital pathway does cortical organization shift from retinotopic to shape-based representation?
key_assumptions: extrastriate responses inherit signals from earlier retinotopic areas
neural similarity in fMRI patterns reflects representational content
shape similarity can be decomposed into low-level vs curvature-based measures
manipulations: shape exemplar varied across stimulus pairs
shape complexity varied across stimulus pairs
ROI compared across retinotopic (V1-V3, V4, VO-1/2) and functional (LO-1, LO-2, LO) areas
key_findings: V1-V3 similarity predicted by low-level but not abstract stimulus measures
LO similarity predicted by abstract but not low-level measures (double dissociation)
LO-2 patterns most predicted by abstract curvature measures, akin to LO
LO-1 patterns predicted by both low-level and abstract measures
interpretations: LO-1 may mark a transitional stage between retinotopic and shape-based organizational principles
ventral stream through V4 and VO-1/2 does not show the same retinotopic-to-abstract transition
